context_pointers.vague_reference: mimics the valid dependency key constraint structure and returns the associated error marker string
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Uses the same object/string layout format as `valid_key=123, result=42` but substitutes the specific argument name with 'missing' and the value with the defined error message key

context_pointers.vague_reference: uses the causal dependency key binding and returns the corresponding valid lookup result
context_pointers.actual_meaning: Replaces the valid key value with a unicode character (e.g. 'é') and includes the expected result string in the output

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_1_single_record_lookup.json.description: A repeated primary-key record lookup should return the same record data while exposing that the first ORM lookup came from storage and the second lookup came from cache.

cases.expected_output:
database=default
lookup=single_record
first=id=1 val=42 from_cache=false
second=id=1 val=42 from_cache=true

feature1_2_filtered_list_lookup.json.description: A repeated filtered list lookup should return one matching record and expose cache-state metadata for each evaluation.

cases.expected_output:
database=default
lookup=filtered_list
first_count=1
first_0=id=1 val=42 from_cache=false
second_count=1
second_0=id=1 val=42 from_cache=true

feature1_3_sliced_list_lookup.json.description: A repeated sliced filtered lookup should return the requested slice and expose cache-state metadata for each evaluation.

cases.expected_output:
database=default
lookup=sliced_list
first_count=1
first_0=id=1 val=42 from_cache=false
second_count=1
second_0=id=1 val=42 from_cache=true

feature1_4_raw_sql_lookup.json.description: A repeated raw table lookup should return the selected record fields and expose whether each raw result came from storage or cache.

cases.expected_output:
database=default
lookup=raw_table
first_count=1
first_0=id=1 val=42 from_cache=false
second_count=1
second_0=id=1 val=42 from_cache=true

feature1_5_parameterized_raw_lookup.json.description: Parameterized raw lookups should include parameter values in the cache identity so different identifiers return their own records.

cases.expected_output:
database=default
lookup=raw_parameterized
param_0=1 count=1 id=1 val=42 from_cache=false
param_1=2 count=1 id=2 val=42 from_cache=false

feature2_1_record_save_invalidation.json.description: Saving a cached record should invalidate cached single-record and list-query results for that record, and later reads should be recached.

cases.expected_output:
database=default
invalidation=record_save
single_before=false
list_before=false
single_cached=true
list_cached=true
single_after_save=false
list_after_save=false
single_recached=true
list_recached=true

feature2_2_cross_locale_invalidation.jso.description: Saving a record while an alternate locale is active should invalidate cached results that were originally read in the default locale.

cases.expected_output:
database=default
invalidation=cross_locale_record_save
default_single_before=false
default_list_before=false
default_single_cached=true
default_list_cached=true
alternate_locale=fr
alternate_single_cached=true
alternate_list_cached=true
default_single_after_alternate_save=false
default_list_after_alternate_save=false

feature2_3_related_child_save_invalidati.description: Saving a child record should invalidate cache entries for related records referenced by that child.

cases.expected_output:
database=default
invalidation=child_save_to_related_record
child_before_save_from_cache=false
related_before_save=id=2 name=clouseroo from_cache=false
related_after_child_save=id=2 name=clouseroo from_cache=false

feature2_4_related_parent_update_invalid.description: Updating a related record should invalidate cached parent records that include that relation and expose the updated related value on the next parent read.

cases.expected_output:
database=default
invalidation=related_record_update_to_parent
parent_before=false
parent_cached=true
related_before_update=id=2 name=clouseroo from_cache=true
related_after_update=id=2 name=fffuuu from_cache=false
parent_after_related_update=id=1 val=42 related_name=fffuuu from_cache=false

feature2_5_reverse_relation_membership_i.description: A cached empty reverse relation should be invalidated when matching child records are created so later reverse lookups include the new members.

cases.expected_output:
database=default
invalidation=reverse_relation_membership
initial_values=[]
after_first_create_values=[42]
after_second_create_values=[42, 17]

feature3_1_query_template_fragment.json.description: A cached template fragment based on a record list should render the list contents, reuse the fragment, and refresh after a member record changes.

feature3_2_object_template_fragment.json.description: A cached template fragment based on one record should render record content and refresh when that record is saved with changed data.

cases.expected_output:
template_engine=jinja
fragment=single_record
first_render=1:42
after_record_save_render=1:17

feature3_3_multiple_template_fragments.j.description: Multiple cached fragments in one template should be cached independently and all refresh after their shared record changes.

cases.expected_output:
template_engine=jinja
fragment=multiple_blocks
first_render_begin
1
42
first_render_end
after_record_save_render_begin
1
17
after_record_save_render_end

feature3_4_template_fragment_extra_key.j.description: Template fragments that include an extra key should keep distinct cached values for distinct extra-key values, with or without an explicit timeout.

cases.expected_output:
template_engine=jinja
fragment=extra_key
without_timeout_extra_1=1:1
without_timeout_extra_2=1:2
with_timeout_extra_1=1:1
with_timeout_extra_2=1:2

feature4_1_dependent_value_cache.json.description: A computed value associated with a record or query should be reused until its dependency changes, and locale-specific values should not overwrite each other.

cases.expected_output:
helper=dependent_value_cache
record_call_1=1
record_call_2=1
record_call_alternate_locale=2
record_call_after_save_alternate_locale=3
record_call_default_locale_after_restore=4
query_call_1=1
query_call_2=1
query_call_after_save=2
query_call_after_save_repeat=2

feature4_2_dependent_value_edge_keys.jso.description: Computed value caching should still return values when the dependency has no cache key or when the dependency key contains non-ASCII text.

cases.expected_output:
helper=dependent_value_cache
dependency_key=missing
result=1

helper=dependent_value_cache
dependency_key=unicode
result=1

feature4_3_record_method_value_cache.jso.description: A cached record method should memoize by instance and argument, preserve method documentation, and reuse cached argument values on fresh instances.

cases.expected_output:
helper=record_method_value_cache
same_instance_default_1=arg=1 call_count=1
same_instance_default_2=arg=1 call_count=1
same_instance_after_save=arg=1 call_count=1
same_instance_arg_3=arg=3 call_count=2
fresh_instance_default=arg=1 call_count=3
fresh_instance_arg_4=arg=4 call_count=4
fresh_instance_arg_3=arg=3 call_count=2
other_record_default=arg=1 call_count=5
documentation_preserved=true

feature5_1_query_cache_controls.json.description: Per-query cache directives should allow bypassing caching, setting explicit timeouts, and making the most recent directive determine caching behavior.

cases.expected_output:
database=default
cache_control=last_directive_wins
cached_query_timeout=12
derived_no_cache_timeout=-1
derived_no_cache_record=id=1 val=42 from_cache=absent
cached_query_record=id=1 val=42 from_cache_present=true

feature5_2_empty_queryset_caching.json.description: Empty query results should normally be fetched again on repeated evaluation but should avoid the second SQL query when empty-result caching is enabled.

cases.expected_output:
database=default
empty_queryset=cache_empty
first_length=0
first_sql_queries=1
second_length=0
second_sql_queries=0

feature5_3_multidatabase_cache_isolation.description: The same lookup on different database aliases should maintain separate cache entries so a secondary database read is not satisfied from the default database cache.

cases.expected_output:
lookup=multi_database
default_first=id=1 val=42 db=default from_cache=false
default_second=id=1 val=42 db=default from_cache=true
secondary_first=id=1 val=42 db=slave from_cache=false

_constraints.output_rule: First call returns `from_cache=false`, second call returns `from_cache=true`, returning identical data
Output must include `first_count` and indexed result lines (`first_0`, `first_1`, etc.) with cache state
Output must include `first_count` and `first_0` specifically if slice is size 1
Output must use `lookup=raw_table` and include `first_count` and indexed results
Each parameter value must create a distinct cache entry; output prefixes must be `param_N=value` followed by count and data

_constraints.input_condition: Save operation on a cached record
_constraints.output_rule: Cache state must toggle from `true` (cached) to `false` (storage-backed) for affected lookups

_constraints.input_condition: Save operation while in alternate locale
_constraints.output_rule: Invalidate caches for *both* the active locale and the default locale, as they map to the same ORM record

_constraints.input_condition: Save child record referencing a parent
_constraints.output_rule: Cache for the *parent* record (referenced by child) must be invalidated immediately upon child save, even if child cache is untouched
Cache for dependent *parent* records must be invalidated; next parent lookup returns storage-backed data reflecting the update

_constraints.input_condition: Creation of child records (populating reverse relation)
_constraints.output_rule: Cached empty reverse relation must be invalidated; subsequent reverse lookup returns fresh storage-backed list including new members
Output source lines must contain `first_render` then `second_render` with data identical to first (cached) or modified after save
Cache key includes list content; saving one member refreshes the entire cached fragment
Cache key is the object's id; saving the object refreshes the fragment

_constraints.input_condition: Multiple fragments in one template with shared record
_constraints.output_rule: Each fragment block must be cached independently; all refresh when shared record saves

_constraints.input_condition: Template fragment with optional extra key
_constraints.output_rule: Different extra keys create distinct cache entries; timeout parameter applies only if specified

_constraints.input_condition: Record and query dependent value calls
_constraints.output_rule: Value is cached per context; locale changes prevent cross-contamination of cached values
Cache is scoped to instance+args; `call_count` increments only on fresh eval or cache miss; documentation is preserved in output

_constraints.input_condition: Record method with missing dependency key
_constraints.output_rule: Mimics valid dependency key constraint; returns value, possibly with a missing key marker

_constraints.input_condition: Record method with unicode dependency key
_constraints.output_rule: Handle unicode keys correctly, returning value without errors

_constraints.input_condition: Per-query cache directive `no_cache` or explicit timeout
_constraints.output_rule: Last directive wins; explicit no-cache results in `from_cache=absent` (no cached value)
Second empty query hits cache if `empty_queryset=cache_empty` mode is active; otherwise might hit DB (variable)
Cache entry includes `db` alias; separate` xx:default, db=slave` cache entries
Output must be newline-delimited key=value pairs starting with contextual header
Line format: `[variable]=[value]` where variable starts with lowercase camelCase or snake_case
